title: rubus hirtus subvar. coloratus
intro: Rubus hirtus subvar. coloratus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a type of thorny shrub native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, fever, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus subvar. coloratus include its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and roots, and then steeping them in hot water or mixing with a solvent.
